metadata.title: Build a Memory Verdict Module with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. Flash holds the weights, SRAM holds the activations — they fail for different reasons, and a single out-of-memory error hides which one.

props.command: claude "write memory.py: inputs param_count, dtype, firmware_kb, largest_activation, target flash_kb + sram_kb. Emit Verdict(fits, flash_margin, sram_margin, mitigations[])."
props.segment: Build a Memory Verdict Module with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: The book's SRAM overflow diagram. Our memory-verdict script tells you exactly how much headroom you have — or how far you're over — before you flash the device.

beats.act: MANIM — Flash bar: FP32 overshoots 2 MB ceiling
beats.narration_text: Fill the flash bar. 1.5 million parameters at float32 — 4 bytes each — equals 6 megabytes. The target has 2 megabytes of flash. The bar crashes through the ceiling. Flash fails. The model cannot be stored.

beats.act: MANIM — SRAM bar: 0.6 MB passes 1 MB ceiling
beats.narration_text: Fill the SRAM bar. Largest activation tensor: 0.6 megabytes. The target has 1 megabyte of SRAM. The bar stays under the ceiling. SRAM passes. The activations fit during inference — but it doesn't matter yet, because the model can't even be loaded.

beats.act: MANIM — int8 toggle: flash drops, both pass, verdict FITS
beats.narration_text: Toggle to INT8. Flash drops from 6 megabytes to 1.5 — under the 2 megabyte ceiling. SRAM is unchanged: 0.6 megabytes, still passing. Both bars are green. The verdict: FITS. The fix was quantization. Not a different chip. The module tells you which memory failed and which mitigation to apply.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write memory.py — inputs: parameter count, dtype, firmware kilobytes, largest activation, and target flash and SRAM kilobytes. Emit a verdict with a fits flag, flash margin, SRAM margin, and a list of mitigations. The verdict distinguishes a flash problem from an SRAM problem — they have different fixes. Add the INT8 toggle and see which metric it moves.

props.command: claude "write memory.py: inputs param_count, dtype, firmware_kb, largest_activation, target flash_kb + sram_kb. Emit Verdict(fits, flash_margin, sram_margin, mitigations[])."

props.title: Build a Memory Verdict Module with Claude Code
